content.problemStatement:
You are given an **unsigned** 323232-bit integer XXX. Find the integer formed after reversing the 323232-bit binary string of XXX.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 323232-bit integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number formed after reversing the 323232-bit binary string of XXX.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single unsigned 32‑bit integer X.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with the decimal value obtained after reversing the 32‑bit binary representation of X.

solution.approach: For each number we treat it as a 32‑bit value. Starting with result = 0, we iterate 32 times: shift result left by one, add the current least‑significant bit of X (X & 1), then shift X right by one. After the loop result contains the bits of X in reverse order. This runs in O(32) = O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) because each test case processes a constant 32 bits.
